title: canMutateCard does not enforce pending-confirmation gate required by spec § 5 for unblock and complete operations
description: Spec § 5 mandates that POST /api/v1/cards/:id/unblock and POST /api/v1/goal-spaces/:id/complete must be blocked when a pending human confirmation exists. canMutateCard has no hasPendingConfirmation check.
evidence: canMutateCard(actor, ctx) — only checks role, goalSpaceInitiatorId match, member, or assignedTo.
recommendation: Add a hasPendingConfirmation field to CardContext (or a dedicated canUnblockCard function) mirroring the execute gate.

title: canDecideConfirmation does not verify the confirmation is in 'pending' status
description: canDecideConfirmation only checks actor.role==='initiator' && actor.id===ctx.goalSpaceInitiatorId. The confirmation status is not part of ConfirmationContext.
note_after_verify: Verified — code only checks role + id match; no status check.
recommendation: Add confirmationStatus to ConfirmationContext and gate canDecideConfirmation on status === 'pending'.

title: ai_role actor is recorded for human-initiated triggers (human_reject, human_confirm), losing audit attribution
description: All 'from-anything -> blocked' rules with human_reject declare actor: 'ai_role'. The trigger human_reject semantically means a human rejected, so audit should record actor=human.
evidence: CARD_TRANSITIONS lines 79-101 all have actor: 'ai_role' for human_reject triggers; line 72-75 review->done actor=ai_role with human_confirm trigger.
note_after_verify: Real but nuanced. The 'actor' field semantics in a state machine is who performed the transition, not who triggered it; current mapping is wrong for human_* triggers. Needs spec clarification (ADR recommended).
recommendation: Model per-(from,to,trigger) actor. For 'human_reject' trigger, actor should be 'human'.

title: human_confirm_timeout transition to 'cancelled' contradicts ai_agent_contracts.md § 10
description: Spec § 10: 'Confirmation timeout must keep or move the card to blocked.' Code defines 'blocked -> cancelled' with trigger 'human_confirm_timeout' — directly conflicts.
note_after_verify: Verified — spec text is verbatim 'Confirmation timeout must keep or move the card to blocked'.
recommendation: Remove 'human_confirm_timeout' from the blocked->cancelled triggers array.

title: canReadGoalSpace returns false for chain_user and viewer even when they have node-board membership, contradicting spec § 3
description: Spec § 3: 'Goal Space: 发起人可管理自己创建的目标空间;其他用户必须通过节点成员关系获得访问权'. Current code unconditionally returns false for non-initiator.
evidence: canReadGoalSpace line 19-22: 'if (actor.role === "initiator") return actor.id === ctx.initiatorId; return false;'
note_after_verify: Documented in code as S2 scope boundary.
recommendation: Accept NodeBoardContext into GoalSpaceContext and union membership check.

title: canExecuteCard grants execution to any chain_user with member or assignedTo access, ignoring the card's current state
description: canExecuteCard allows a chain_user to fire POST /cards/:id/execute on cards in any state including done/cancelled/blocked. State machine catches it, but AI compute is already billed.
evidence: ExecuteCardContext has only card + hasPendingConfirmation; no card state.
recommendation: Add currentState to ExecuteCardContext and require state in {backlog, todo, dev, review, blocked}.

title: Goal space canGoalSpaceTransition returns true for active->completed even when preconditions are unsatisfied
description: canGoalSpaceTransition('active', 'completed') unconditionally returns true. Preconditions (pending, blocked, all-done) only checked by assertGoalSpaceTransition.
evidence: canGoalSpaceTransition line 95 returns true without inspecting opts.
recommendation: Either rename canGoalSpaceTransition to clarify static-only, or accept optional opts and return false on unmet preconditions.

title: runWithAudit realtime_events sequence MAX+1 subquery is documented as concurrency-safe but breaks on async drivers
description: With better-sqlite3 (synchronous, single connection), this is safe. With async drivers, two concurrent transactions could both read the same MAX and conflict on the unique index.
evidence: sql`(SELECT COALESCE(MAX(sequence), 0) + 1 FROM realtime_events WHERE goal_space_id = ${ctx.goalSpaceId})`
note_after_verify: Forward-looking only; current code is correct under better-sqlite3 serial model.
recommendation: When migrating to PostgreSQL, switch to a per-goalSpaceId row lock or server-side SEQUENCE.

title: canReadCard allows viewer to read any card via assignedTo, but spec § 4 ambiguity
description: canReadCard returns true for any role where assignedTo === actor.id. The function docstring says viewer.read=member-only, but the code grants via assignedTo.
recommendation: Make viewer.read path explicit. Either (a) split into canReadCardAssignedTo requiring role != viewer, or (b) add a test pinning the intended behavior.

title: Self-loop transitions can be fired repeatedly with no idempotency check
description: Four self-loop rules (backlog->backlog, todo->todo, dev->dev, review->review) allow unbounded state_transitions rows.
evidence: Lines 137-160 declare self-loops with no precondition; assertTransition doesn't inspect details.
recommendation: Document expected caller behavior, or add call-site check that the relevant field actually changed.

title: actorType 'system' has no matching authorization function; system-driven operations must impersonate a user
description: The Actor type carries roles 'initiator'|'chain_user'|'viewer'. No 'system' actor. System cancel must impersonate initiator, blurring the audit trail.
evidence: ActorRole = UserRole; no system path in any can* function.
recommendation: Add a separate code path for system-driven operations (e.g., withInternalActor that bypasses authz but writes actorType='system').

title: ConfirmationContext carries nodeBoardMemberIds but canDecideConfirmation does not use them
description: ConfirmationContext includes nodeBoardMemberIds but canDecideConfirmation only inspects goalSpaceInitiatorId.
evidence: ConfirmationContext at types.ts:79-85; canDecideConfirmation at confirmation.ts:15-17.
recommendation: Either remove the dead field or document the future role it plays.
